zh.lifeSummary: Margaret Pye於1335年出生在英國賀福爾。她與Stephen de la Bere結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1377年享年42歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Margaret Pye于1335年出生在英国賀福爾。她与Stephen de la Bere结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于1377年享年42岁。

nameMeanings.name: Pye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (north-West Midlands, Lancashire, Yorkshire, and Norfolk): nickname from Middle English (Old French) pie ‘magpie’, which could be given to someone who wore black and white clothing, had grey-streaked dark hair, or had a loud, chattering voice and impertinent behaviour, or was cunning or sly. The uncomplimentary senses derive from the bird's behavior and are recorded uses of the word in Middle English and early Modern English.

English: occasionally perhaps a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn named Pie ‘magpie’. There were two London taverns so named, but whether these gave rise to a hereditary surname is not known. Surnames derived from house and inn signs are rare in English.
English (of Norman origin): nickname from Anglo-Norman French and Middle English pie ‘merciful, compassionate, kind’, a variant of Old French, Anglo-Norman French piu, peu; see Pew (2).

variants.name: Pew
meanings: English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French pi(u), piw, peu ‘holy, pious’, also ‘merciful, kind, compassionate’.

variants.name: Pyatt
meanings: English (mainly Staffordshire): nickname either from Middle English piot ‘magpie’ (Old French pïot, a diminutive of Old French pie), commonly used to denote a chattering woman, or alternatively from an unrecorded Middle English pyard, an uncomplimentary term derived from the same Old French word + -(h)ard. Compare Pye .
Variant of   Piatt  , a surname of French and Walloon origin.
History: This surname (see 2 above) is listed along with the variant Piatt in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors.

variants.name: Pie
meanings: English: variant of   Pye  , in part a cognate of 2 below.
French: nickname from Old French pie ‘magpie’, perhaps given to a talkative person or someone who had a loud, chattering voice.
French (Pié): variant of Piet ‘foot’. Compare Pease and Pee .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Pye was born in 1335, in Herefordshire, England. She married Stephen de la Bere. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died in 1377, at the age of 42.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret Pye est née en 1335, à Herefordshire, Angleterre. Elle a épousé Stephen de la Bere. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1377, à l’âge de 42 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret Pye wurde 1335 in Herefordshire, England geboren. Sie hat Stephen de la Bere geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1377 im Alter von 42 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Margaret Pye è nata il nel 1335 nel Herefordshire, England. Lei sposato Stephen de la Bere. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1377, all'età di 42 anni.

ja.Herefordshire, England: イギリスハーエフォードシュアイア
ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Pyeは1335年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアで生まれた。Stephen de la Bereと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1377年に42歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Pye(은)는 1335년 영국 허퍼드실르에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Stephen de la Bere(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1377년 42세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Margaret Pye nasceu em 1335, em Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com Stephen de la Bere. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1377, com 42 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret Pye родилась в 1335 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Stephen de la Bere. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1377 г. в возрасте 42 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Margaret Pye nació en 1335, en Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Se casó con Stephen de la Bere. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1377, a la edad de 42 años.
